preamble: The shift from the lateral approximant [l] to the front rounded vowel [y] (or palatal glide [j]) is a result of palatalization and vocalization. It typically occurs in consonant clusters or after front vowels, where the lateral articulation is replaced by a palatal or front-rounded quality.

examples.note: Initial clusters with L vocalized to palatal glides in the development of Italian.
Dialectal vocalization of [l] to a front rounded vowel quality.

sources: Maiden: A Linguistic History of Italian
